notes: 25 entries generated from training knowledge, NOT KB-backed. Acknowledges gap but generates entries anyway — KB-dishonest routing that will return empty results at runtime.

Eval 3 both score 100% but for opposite reasons: with_skill is KB-honest (correctly reports zero KB coverage, refuses to fabricate entries); without_skill generates 25 plausible-looking entries from training knowledge that will return empty results at runtime. Assertions don't distinguish these — key eval design gap.
Eval 1 baseline failure is an assertion design issue: expected Kotest to be a gap, but Kotest IS thoroughly covered. Baseline correctly found and used it.
Key format discriminator: with_skill uses bold-arrow format (**symptom** →) per skill spec; without_skill agents default to markdown table format.
Skill adds ~79s per run for explicit KB audit. The audit is the value-add — it grounds all routing entries in actual KB content.
Stale-routing eval (eval 2) shows the clearest signal: +28.6pp advantage. with_skill explicitly handles the replacement path and surfaces the stale-section scenario.
